title: CV
text: I’m a real-world evidence (RWE) and AI scientist with over five years’ experience generating regulatory-relevant evidence across the drug development lifecycle. My work combines observational study design and causal inference — target trial emulation, external control arms, comparative effectiveness — with the applied use of AI and machine learning to accelerate and standardise evidence generation. I’m based in Barcelona, Spain.

title: CV
text:
Experience

RWE Associate Director
AstraZeneca, Barcelona · February 2025 – present
Leading real-world epidemiology across women’s cancers (breast, ovarian, endometrial) within Clinical Data Sciences, Oncology Data Science and AI, in Oncology R&D.

Shaping RWE generation strategies supporting phase II/III pipeline investment decisions and regulatory strategy, partnering with R&D, Clinical Science, and Global Medical Affairs.
Designing and delivering observational studies and external control arms, applying target trial emulation, propensity-score matching, IPTW, and clone-censor-weight methods to contextualise clinical trial and competitor results.
Co-designed an end-to-end agentic AI pipeline for automated RWE analyses, accelerating study delivery and standardising phenotyping, endpoint definition, and analytical workflows.
Leading Claude Code and AI-development training for the team and line management, and mentoring a colleague in agentic AI development.
Participating in AstraZeneca’s AI Explorers programme, contributing to enterprise efforts to evaluate and embed AI across evidence-generation workflows.



Principal RWE Statistical Programmer
Daiichi Sankyo UK, London (remote) · July 2024 – February 2025
First EU hire for the US-based RWE programming team, working internationally across functions on metastatic breast cancer and advanced non-small cell lung cancer.

Served as statistical programming study lead, collaborating with biostatisticians, clinical scientists, and programmers to deliver tables, figures, and listings using Flatiron, ConcertAI, and CPRD data (as a nominated CPRD user).
Built and deployed internal tooling to expedite study development: R Shiny applications, R packages, and data pipelines — standardising cohort-identification algorithms (e.g. HER2-low populations) and automating TFL production and patient-journey viewers.
Advised on data source selection and cohort generation, and built cross-functional relationships across biometrics, medical evidence, and HEOR.



RWE Data Scientist (contract)
AstraZeneca, Cambridge (remote) · September 2022 – July 2024
Working with Global Medical Affairs and Clinical Scientist teams in oncology (primarily breast cancer) to address key evidence gaps.

Led multiple concurrent RWE studies from protocol design through analysis to publication, using large EHR and claims datasets (Flatiron, Tempus, ConcertAI) in R and SQL.
Advised on statistical protocols and evaluated data assets for fitness-for-purpose across study designs, contributing to data source recommendations for new research programmes.
Provided R subject-matter expertise within a mixed R and SAS programming team, and mentored analysts across simultaneous oncology studies.
Recognised with multiple internal AstraZeneca “spotlight” awards for performance and stakeholder feedback.



Post-doctoral Data Analyst, EAVE II
Usher Institute, University of Edinburgh · March 2022 – September 2022
Working with large, confidential NHS EHR datasets in a secure environment — developing protocols, implementing epidemiological models and matching algorithms, and communicating results through presentations and academic papers.

COVID-19 in Pregnancy in Scotland (COPS): reproductive epidemiology and data analysis on maternal health and outcomes.
EAVE II: disease forecasting, statistical and mathematical modelling.



Clinical Studies Administrator / Assistant
Wales Cancer Trials Unit, Centre for Trials Research · 2016 – 2017, 2019 (part-time)

title: CV
text:
Education

PhD, Ecology and Evolutionary Biology
University of Edinburgh · 2019 – 2025 (viva defence completed; awarded 2026)
Thesis on resource-mediated effects on wild wood mouse networks, behaviour, and demographics — combining fieldwork with social network analysis, parasite epidemiology, observational data, and statistical and mathematical modelling. Developed pipelines for processing large-scale remote sensing data. Fully funded through NERC and the E4 Doctoral Training Programme.


BSc (Hons) Biological Sciences (Ecology), First Class
Cardiff University · 2015 – 2019
Specialising in infection biology and epidemiology, systems biology and modelling, and global change biology. Awarded the school prize for best dissertation research. Included a placement year.

See Publications for peer-reviewed work and conference abstracts.

title: Sam Hillman
text:
I’m a real-world evidence (RWE) and AI scientist working in oncology drug development. I’m an RWE Associate Director at AstraZeneca in Barcelona, within Clinical Data Sciences in Oncology Data Science and AI, where I lead real-world epidemiology across women’s cancers — breast, ovarian, and endometrial.
Most of my work is about generating regulatory-relevant evidence from electronic health record and claims data: designing and delivering observational studies and external control arms, and applying causal inference methods — target trial emulation, propensity-score matching, IPTW, clone-censor-weight — to contextualise clinical trials and inform investment and regulatory decisions.
Alongside that, I work on the applied use of AI in evidence generation. I co-designed an end-to-end agentic pipeline for automated RWE analyses, and I lead AI-development training for my team. I hold a PhD from the University of Edinburgh on resource-mediated effects in wild wood mouse networks, which combined fieldwork with social network analysis, parasite epidemiology, and statistical modelling — the observational-data problems turn out to rhyme more than you’d expect.
I’ve published in The Lancet Respiratory Medicine, Nature Communications, The Lancet Regional Health – Europe, and JCO Precision Oncology, and I work primarily in R.






I’m based in Barcelona, Spain. The best way to reach me is by email or LinkedIn.
